data.title: Betsson Steps Up EUR 40m Buyback With New B-Share Purchases
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Betsson AB ( ($SE:BETS.B) ) has shared an update.

Betsson AB has repurchased 140,000 of its own series B shares on Nasdaq Stockholm between 7 and 10 April 2026, at a weighted average price of SEK 101.86 and a total value of about SEK 14.26 million. The transactions were executed by Arctic Securities on behalf of Betsson under the company’s ongoing share buyback programme of up to EUR 40 million, which runs until 30 April 2026 and follows EU safe-harbour rules.

Following these latest purchases, Betsson holds 4,719,942 series B shares and 2,747,433 series C shares out of a total 142,729,838 shares outstanding, tightening its free float while signalling continued confidence in its valuation and capital position. The accumulated volume under the buyback programme has reached 3,339,600 series B shares at a total cost of SEK 382.36 million, underscoring the company’s active approach to shareholder returns and balance-sheet optimisation.

More about Betsson AB

Betsson AB is a Stockholm-listed holding company focused on fast-growing businesses in the online gaming industry. The group is one of the leading global online gaming operators and aims to outpace market growth both organically and via acquisitions while maintaining profitability and sustainability. Its shares are traded on Nasdaq Stockholm’s Large Cap segment under the BETS-B ticker.

Average Trading Volume: 1,003,177

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: SEK12.44B

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Wagners Holding Co. Ltd. ( (AU:WGN) ) has shared an update.

Wagners Holding Company director Denis Wagner has updated his disclosed interest in the company following a modest on-market share purchase. On 9 April 2026, Wagner acquired 24,972 ordinary shares at $4.00 each, lifting his total relevant interest from 60,029,351 to 60,054,323 shares across a combination of direct holdings and entities he controls.

The notice details that Wagner’s indirect interests include shares held via family trusts and entities, as well as a significant relevant interest arising from a shareholders’ pre-emption deed with other members of the Wagner family. This incremental increase in his already substantial stake reinforces the strong alignment between the director and other major family shareholders, underlining continued insider confidence and concentrated ownership in the company.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:WGN) stock is a Buy with a A$5.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Wagners Holding Co. Ltd. stock, see the AU:WGN Stock Forecast page.

More about Wagners Holding Co. Ltd.

Wagners Holding Company Limited is an Australian company listed on the ASX, operating in the construction materials and related services sector. The company’s activities typically encompass the production and supply of construction materials, infrastructure services, and associated products to domestic and potentially international markets.

data.title: Anemoi Revamps Trasna Takeover Terms, Plans Share Consolidation and Warrant Offer
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Anemoi International Limited ( (GB:AMOI) ) has issued an announcement.

Anemoi International has signed an amended binding agreement to acquire 100% of Trasna Solutions FZ LLC via a reverse takeover that values Trasna at an enterprise value of $150 million, with consideration to be paid in new Anemoi shares priced at up to £0.02. The deal, which remains subject to due diligence, fundraise and regulatory approvals, will also see Anemoi seek readmission of the enlarged group to the London Stock Exchange’s Main Market and change its name to Trasna Ltd.

To manage the substantial increase in share count from the transaction, the board is considering a 1-for-100 share consolidation aimed at lifting the share price and improving marketability to a broader investor base. In parallel, existing warrant holders are being offered a voluntary surrender-and-subscription arrangement at £0.02 per share that could raise up to about £1.9 million, reduce perceived stock overhang, and strengthen the balance sheet ahead of completion of the reverse takeover.

Spark’s Take on AMOI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, AMOI is a Neutral.

The score is primarily held down by weak financial performance (declining small revenue, ongoing losses, and persistent cash burn). Technicals are a partial offset with price trading above key moving averages and a positive MACD, but valuation support is limited due to negative earnings and no dividend data.

To see Spark’s full report on AMOI stock, click here.

More about Anemoi International Limited

Anemoi International Ltd is a London-listed company whose shares trade on the Main Market of the London Stock Exchange in the Equity Shares (Commercial Companies) category. The group is repositioning itself through a reverse takeover of Trasna Solutions FZ LLC, after which it plans to rebrand as Trasna Ltd and pursue growth under the enlarged structure.

Average Trading Volume: 375,646

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Learn more about AMOI stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

Kingfisher ( (GB:KGF) ) has issued an announcement.

Kingfisher plc has continued executing its previously announced £300 million capital return programme, repurchasing 500 ordinary shares for cancellation on 10 April 2026 via an on-exchange transaction with BNP Paribas SA. The modest buyback tranche underscores the group’s ongoing effort to return surplus capital to shareholders, which can enhance earnings per share and signals management’s confidence in the balance sheet and long-term cash generation.

The transaction was carried out in line with London Stock Exchange rules and the UK version of the EU Market Abuse Regulation, with detailed trade data made available through the exchange’s disclosure platform. For investors, the programme provides incremental support to the share price and clarifies Kingfisher’s capital allocation priorities at a time when retailers face a challenging consumer and macroeconomic backdrop.

data.title: Sosandar Swings to Profit as Revenue Climbs and Margins Strengthen
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Sosandar PLC ( (GB:SOS) ) has shared an announcement.

Sosandar reported a strong FY26 performance, with total revenue rising 14% to £42.3m and own-site sales up 24%, supported by higher traffic, better conversion, and increased orders from both new and existing customers. The brand continued to rank among the top sellers for third-party partners such as NEXT and saw trading with Marks & Spencer normalise after a prior cyber incident, underscoring the resilience and appeal of its proposition.

Profitability improved, with a swing to a £0.4m profit before tax and gross margin expanding to 63.9%, while net cash increased to £8.4m despite share buybacks, highlighting the business’s cash-generative profile. Physical stores delivered better performance in their second year, especially in market towns, but still weigh on overall profits, leading the company to pause further openings as it focuses on driving location-level profitability and sustainable, profitable growth from its existing footprint.

Spark’s Take on SOS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SOS is a Neutral.

Sosandar PLC’s stock score is driven by positive technical indicators and encouraging corporate events, despite financial and valuation challenges. The company’s strategic initiatives and insider confidence provide a positive outlook, but profitability and cash flow issues remain significant concerns.

To see Spark’s full report on SOS stock, click here.

More about Sosandar PLC

Sosandar plc is a UK-based womenswear retailer focused on fashion-forward, affordable clothing for style-conscious women who have moved on from lower-quality, price-led brands. The company designs and sells predominantly own-label products across all key fashion categories via Sosandar.com, its own stores, and partnerships with major retailers such as NEXT and Marks & Spencer.

Founded in 2016 and listed on AIM in 2017, Sosandar targets an underserved segment seeking quality, trend-led garments that feel sexy, feminine, and chic. Its growth strategy centres on data-driven product development, lifestyle marketing, and expanding both direct-to-consumer and third-party channels to reach customers wherever they prefer to shop.

data.title: Tharisa boosts chrome output and launches underground mining as PGM grades dip
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Tharisa ( (GB:THS) ) has issued an update.

Tharisa reported mixed operating results for the second quarter of FY2026, with platinum group metals output down 11.6% to 34.3 koz due to lower reef grades, but chrome concentrate production up 15.6% to 404 kt on improved feed grades and steady recoveries. Stronger commodity prices boosted the average PGM basket price by nearly 38% and supported chrome pricing, helping the Group lift its cash balance to US$184.3 million despite higher debt linked to underground development and working capital.

Operationally, the quarter was marked by the first blast at the Apollo portal, formally launching underground mining at the Tharisa Mine and underpinning plans to invest over US$500 million in underground expansion over the next decade. The company also advanced early works and funding for its Karo Platinum project in Zimbabwe, while maintaining solid safety metrics and reiterating FY2026 production guidance of 145–165 koz PGMs and 1.50–1.65 Mt of chrome, reinforcing its long-term growth and diversification strategy.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:THS) stock is a Buy with a £220.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Tharisa stock, see the GB:THS Stock Forecast page.

More about Tharisa

Tharisa plc is a Cyprus-incorporated mining and metals company dual-listed in Johannesburg and London, focused on platinum group metals (PGMs) and chrome concentrates. Its flagship Tharisa Mine is transitioning from open pit to combined open pit and underground operations, while the Group is also developing the Karo Platinum project in Zimbabwe, targeting long-life, large-scale PGM and chrome production.

Average Trading Volume: 520,469

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: £350.2M

For an in-depth examination of THS stock, go to TipRanks’ Overview page.

data.title: AB Dynamics absorbs China impairment but holds margins and backs full-year outlook
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

AB Dynamics ( (GB:ABDP) ) has shared an update.

AB Dynamics reported a 16% drop in first-half revenue to £48.8m and a swing to a statutory operating loss, largely driven by delayed customer orders, weaker-than-expected volumes at its VadoTech testing services business in China and a £16.8m exceptional charge, mostly a non-cash impairment. Despite the top-line decline, the company held its adjusted operating margin at 18.6%, maintained strong cash conversion with net cash of £39.3m, raised the interim dividend by 10% and reiterated that full-year adjusted operating profit should meet market expectations, supported by an improving order intake, a £47m order book and structural growth drivers in active safety and autonomous systems.

Management has launched a strategic review of the commoditised VadoTech China operation while sharpening its focus on innovation, aligning its testing products to upcoming regulatory and consumer rating changes and building out its simulation offering, where the Delta S3 Spin simulator is gaining early traction. The group continues to highlight its OEM- and powertrain-agnostic positioning, geographic diversification and pricing power as buffers against industry and geopolitical headwinds, and remains committed to medium-term goals of roughly 10% annual organic growth, operating margins above 20% and strong cash generation for reinvestment.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:ABDP) stock is a Hold with a £1370.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on AB Dynamics stock, see the GB:ABDP Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on ABDP Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ABDP is a Outperform.

AB Dynamics shows strong financial performance and a positive outlook from its recent earnings call, which are the most significant factors in its overall score. However, technical indicators suggest a bearish trend, and the valuation is only moderately attractive, which tempers the overall score.

To see Spark’s full report on ABDP stock, click here.

More about AB Dynamics

AB Dynamics is a UK-headquartered designer, manufacturer and supplier of advanced testing, simulation and measurement products and services for the global transport market. The group serves major automotive manufacturers, Tier 1 suppliers and service providers, whose R&D and testing functions use its systems to verify vehicle dynamics and active safety technologies, including ADAS and autonomous features.

Average Trading Volume: 59,621

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: £262.6M

data.title: Block Energy secures up to $75m Sanning farm-out to appraise strategic Georgian gas project
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Block Energy Plc ( (GB:BLOE) ) has provided an announcement.

Block Energy plc has signed a binding Framework Agreement with Zhijiang Sanning Energy for the farm-out of its Project III gas assets in Georgia, under which Sanning will acquire a 51% stake while Block retains 49% and operatorship. The deal gives Block access to a carry of up to $75 million for appraisal drilling and early gas facilities across the Lower Eocene and Upper Cretaceous horizons, including Patardzueli-Samgori, Rustavi and Teleti.

The agreement secures an initial committed carry of about $13 million to appraise the Patardzueli-Samgori field, with further optional carries for additional appraisal and development work, and potential early gas processing and pipeline infrastructure. Project III holds an estimated 2.77 TCF of 2C contingent gas resources and has been declared strategic by Georgia, positioning Block to accelerate phased appraisal and ramp up gas output near the South Caucasus Pipeline if the programme is successful.

Block will keep 100% ownership of its other Georgian projects and all existing production, limiting dilution to Project III while bringing in a financially strong Chinese partner linked to one of China’s leading chemical groups. Completion of the transaction remains subject to definitive agreements, regulatory and government approvals in Georgia and China, and is targeted for the second half of 2026, with operations expected to start in 2027 upon closing.

Spark’s Take on BLOE Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, BLOE is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by declining revenue and ongoing losses (negative margins and negative ROE), alongside a weak/less-informative valuation signal from the negative P/E. Offsetting factors include a strong capital structure with low leverage and improving cash flow, plus bullish price momentum—though the very high RSI suggests the recent move may be overextended.

To see Spark’s full report on BLOE stock, click here.

More about Block Energy Plc

Block Energy plc is a development and production company focused on oil and gas assets in Georgia, operating multiple projects under the XIB and XIF production sharing contracts. The company targets low-capex, short-cycle gas appraisal and development opportunities near existing infrastructure, while retaining full ownership of its current oil and gas production and other exploration projects.

Average Trading Volume: 6,278,286

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: £13.86M

See more insights into BLOE stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Partners Group Private Equity posts slight February NAV decline amid mixed portfolio revaluations
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Princess Private Equity Holding ( (GB:PEY) ) has issued an update.

Partners Group Private Equity Limited reported a 0.5% decline in net asset value for February 2026, to EUR 12.63 per share or EUR 858.84 million, as portfolio revaluations outweighed favorable currency movements. The drop was driven mainly by weaker share price performance in listed holdings KinderCare Learning Companies and Vishal Mega Mart, and a lower valuation for USIC, despite operational improvements and margin gains at the utility services group.

Partially offsetting these pressures, portfolio companies Rosen Group and pet food maker MPM Products posted valuation gains on the back of stronger financial results and growth, with MPM benefiting from expanding revenues across key markets. PGPE Ltd also continued its strong distribution momentum, receiving EUR 22.2 million from the sale of specialty insurer Convex Group, highlighting ongoing realizations within the portfolio and active value‑creation efforts across core holdings.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:PEY) stock is a Sell with a EUR10.60 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Princess Private Equity Holding stock, see the GB:PEY Stock Forecast page.

More about Princess Private Equity Holding

Partners Group Private Equity Limited is a Guernsey‑domiciled investment holding company focused on private equity direct investments and managed by Partners Group, one of the largest global private markets firms. Listed on the London Stock Exchange under tickers PEY and PEYS, the company targets long‑term capital growth and an attractive dividend yield for shareholders.

Average Trading Volume: 91,240
